tasks.text: Analyze rayAABB_inv for divisionless early-out pattern (same as rayTriangle optimization)
Analyze IntersectBVH to find optimization opportunities
Apply divisionless early-out to rayTriangle (defer division until after barycentric checks)

notes.text: Session resumed at iteration 3. Previous change: rayTriangle divisionless early-out gave 2.0% improvement. Previous failed attempt: AVX2 batched AABB (abandoned due to scatter/gather overhead). Remaining hotspots: IntersectBVH (22% excl), rayAABB_inv (8.6% excl), sampleFace (4.1%), RayBoxItersect (4.2%)

Starting iteration 4. Reviewed flame graph from current bench. Top exclusive hotspots: IntersectBVH (17.67%), rayTriangle (16.53%), rayAABB_inv (8.29%), rayAABB_inv_x2_soa (5.11%), RayBoxItersect (3.65%), sampleFace (3.89%). The rayTriangle optimization is already in place.
rayAABB_inv is already divisionless (uses precomputed invDir). The 'divisionless early-out' pattern from rayTriangle does not directly apply to rayAABB_inv since it has no division to defer.

rayTriangle currently computes f = 1.0f / a immediately after the determinant check, then uses f for u, v, t. This means every ray that passes the |a|>eps check must pay for a division, even though most rays miss on u or v checks. Deferring the division saves ~12% of rayTriangle's own time.
